payload.answer: I can help repair the shorthand, but it’s ambiguous. Likely expansions are "Sam from Goldman likes Bill Evans, apparently" or something similar.
citations: For context, sam frm goldmn likes bill evns apparently
repair_options: Treat "sam frm goldmn" as "Sam from Goldman" and "bill evns" as "Bill Evans".
Keep the shorthand as-is and ask for clarification before saving any memory.
Record it as an uncertain statement with the original wording preserved.
If you meant a different Sam or Bill, provide the full names and I can help disambiguate.
